title: Counting with Predicates
prompt_md:
## Goal

Read a list of integers and report two counts: how many are even, and how many are negative. Do the counting with `std::count_if` and **captureless lambdas** (`[]`) -- one predicate per question. No raw counting loops.

## Input

- Line 1: an integer `N` (1 <= N <= 100).
- Line 2: `N` space-separated integers (-10000 <= each <= 10000).

## Output

Exactly two lines:
```
Even: <count of even values>
Negative: <count of values < 0>
```

title: Custom Sort with Lambdas
prompt_md:
## Goal

Sort a list of integers by **absolute value**, ascending. When two values have the same magnitude, the negative one comes first (so `-3` precedes `3`). Do the sorting with `std::sort` and a **captureless lambda** comparator. Compute magnitude yourself -- do not include `<cmath>`.

## Input

- Line 1: an integer `N` (1 <= N <= 100).
- Line 2: `N` space-separated integers (-10000 <= each <= 10000).

## Output

- The sorted values, space-separated, on one line.
